item.content:encoded: I remember seeing this piece when I was a lurker, and it still holds up. So much backstory and development for this odd, obscure character. Gotta applaud the author for fitting all this into a middle-length piece without anything feeling too forced.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be found under any source as cc-compliant. Thus, it is assumed that the image is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
mr. pierrot clearly has no idea what he's doing. on the other hand, that little girl may well have been murdered by her brother ("my sister was already beat up and sleeping", Mathieu either knocked her out or killed her) if he hadn't gotten her first. of course, given all the kid corpses, at what cost?

I still feel really sad for him. he doesnt want those kids to die. and his only attempted breach in almost 50 years of containment was because he was upset about the idea of children getting bullied. I'm sad for the kids, but for mr pierrot too. :-(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Protecting children regardless of their own wellbeing.
It's only slightly depressing when you try not to think about it!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: my favorite "genre" (i guess?) of stories is starting out cute before becoming really disturbing. the fact that this caught me off-guard just makes me love it even more.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: due to radio silence from the author, I've decided not to do this wikiwalk proposal

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

As mentioned upthread, I imagine this as sort of a "reverse SCP-1076". While SCP-1076 affects parents to take care of it like a parasite, this one affects children to take care of them, but it really doesn't know how.

Editing this:

SCP-1810 then began to strike the door of its cell, demanding to be allowed out, citing that it needed to "protect the babies."

To this:

SCP-1810 then began to strike the door of its cell, demanding to be allowed out, citing that it needed to "protect the babies."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's adorable, creepy, sad and nasty in equal parts.

Mr. Pierrot is not evil, is not cruel, no want to damage the humans, he just want to protect the children, he try to do the best but always failed in his purpose. It is just an innocent monster, unable to understand our world.

… We secure. We contain. But most of all, we protect. This includes those that we have contained. That is all."

take an enthusiastic +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey! There's The Man! +! for perfect Foundation article and also originality in an already inspired story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy and loving at the same time. Quite the interesting ending though, it caught me off-guard after I thought "Holy shit. An SCP that actually care-takes children?"

+1

item.title: Congrats on +250!
item.content:encoded:
This has always been my favorite SCP! From the way it is written, to the way that Mr.Pierrot is just trying his best at something he is not familiar with. This is truly one of the best I have ever read, keep up the good work Vincent_Redgrave!

Also, sorry for bad English, I am still in the class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1076 is the one you are talking about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I know. I forget the number, but it had to do with the "lost child" being the SCP, and it drew enough sympathy from the parents to get them to neglect their own children to death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My emotions are conflicted but wonderfully so. I feel bad for this sad lost soul, but yooooo, he's the world's worst parent (of human kids). Delicious buildup and shifting of emotional response. You really hit the ball out of the park with this one! Showers you with my '+1' if I could upvote a thousand times I would. I WOULD.

item.title: Re: Happy +200
item.content:encoded: AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH 200! Holy shit!

item.title: Happy +200
item.content:encoded: I don't find this "impressive" (because it doesn't try to be and really doesn't need to do so, either), but it's excellently written; Pierrot's personality is handled quite well, as the base concept could lead itself to many pitfalls. It gets a really good balance of showing and telling, making it a rather good read in terms of flow.

item.title: Not exactly creepy, more adorable and sad than that.
item.content:encoded:
I'm two years late, but I myself find this… adorable. This is sad and adorable. It just wants to go home and take care of those children who are in danger and have no one to take care of them.

It's more dark and sad than creepy. It's like the Admirable, kind version of Slenderman.

Except, Mr. Pierrot would kick Slendy's A** two ways from sunday. (Wat?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: creepy picture, feelsy scp T.T

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god what. this is horrifying. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you think it's some sort of rodent (that may or may not have been alive when he tried to cook it), then yes.

item.title: Very good.
item.content:encoded:
A well-intentioned alien being, that honestly wants to help, but screws everything up horrifically.

An excellent example of the road to Hell being paved with good intentions.

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the "weird meat" what I think it is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Unicorn has a cutie mark!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you be more specific? I'm not sure which one this is referring to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is another SCP that is basically the same thing as this, but with the child affecting the host,unlike this one.
Still, good skip, upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awwwww. This was really cute.+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Due to the paternal nature of this SCP, and the "big red bowl" comment during the interview, this def gets an upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took me ages to realise this wasn't going to be a Little Mister. Love it, nevertheless. Sad and creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It took me a month, but I got a French-speaking member of our community to look over this with me, and after some discussion on proper French nomenclature and other issues, Mr. Prewitt is now known as Mr. Pierrot (I'll be editing the listname in a minute), as that's a bit more common, and is actually a French name. Damn Google for misleading me XD

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: I tried to imply that he carved a horn out of some random stick and rammed it into the head.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
As a punishment for this transgression I upvoted your article so that it's no longer at +100.

No seriously - it's not a new concept you pulled out, but it's written well, especially stuff like the unicorn (which I assume was a horse with a horn rammed into its head somehow)

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely not, if you do that we will strip you of your votes.

Seriously though, congratulations!
Can I celebrate my first +100 myself? XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made some minor edits throughout this one.

Also, I used G-Translate for the French in the last paragraph, I would appreciate if someone who actually knows how to speak it could take a look, see if I translated it properly. (I realized I had a French-Speaking alien writing in English, and decided to correct that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…this is a dabus, isn't it?

oh my god

it's true

EDIT: It obviously isn't ripping off Torment, it's just an awesome accidental similarity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought "Oh how cute. Just a big guy caring about taking care of lost children."

But then at the end I was like "What the…? I don't even… Oh my…"

Still, creepy where it was meant to be creepy, and I enjoyed it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm… I'll see what I can work out on that bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article makes me uncomfortable in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept and I like how it comes through in the incident log. The only thing that bothers me is the interview with the child. I'm not feeling like this is how a 6 year-old actually talks or reacts and its enough to break immersion for me. A 6 year-old would know when something is bleeding or if something is hurt and it would probably scare the loving shit out of them seeing a bleeding pony in its death throws. I feel like this interview should have gone a different direction.

You still get a +1 from me though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was only kind of okay until I hit the interview and final addendum, whereupon my reaction can be summed up as "PFFFWHOA".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a Slenderman that protects children, rather than eating them. It's also a very interesting SCP in its own right, rather than being compared to another being.

It's a great example of how trying to help can go wrong.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a problem if it's better than Slenderman, and in some ways, this is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was completely unaware that such a thing existed, though I can see the similarities after reading that. Personally, I was worried that the cries of "SLENDERSITTER" would rise up and engulf us all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed errors and weirdness that Drewbear, Djentlight, Francis Willjam, and DamnCREAM brought up, I think. Anyone see anything else I may have missed/need to address?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait a sec… freakishly tall and thin, doesn't age, unnaturally strong, builds things, is obsessed with doors and gateways potentially opening into another universe if the right key is in its possession…

…this is a dabus, isn't it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah. Yeah, this line was from a previous iteration where he was a combo of Bes and Mr. Rogers. I moved /way/ away from that by the time I got to where he is. It was originally intended to keep them from getting too emotionally attached. I will say that in my headcanon, they could easily pull a chunk of the FFL or something for their security detail.

Also, I'll see if I can fit physical dimensions in the article somehow. I've tried it a couple different ways and it hasn't fit properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this idea. I'll work something out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there a way we can compromise and add a translator's note that there is a level of inaccuracy in the translation due to the girl's simplistic french, being 6 and everything, and a more accurate translation based on not onlywhat the girl was saying but the inflection of her voice and reflecting her simplistic words can be found in another document page? That sounds like something the SCPFoundation would do, both for efficiency and accuracy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree that the "no more than a week" thing isn't really explained, but finding agents/researchers who speak French? The Foundation is (theoretically) a global organization. Why wouldn't they have a sizable presence in France? Heck, in-universe there's the French Human Beehive. And out-of-universe, we have the Fondat SCP French translation site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"and I can't honestly say it would sound very much like a six year old girl if I wrote out her enunciating everything she said, yknow?"

Maybe if it was in its original French. For a translation though, and especially for an SCP file, that seems to me like the way it should be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall excellent, upvoted.

Minor quibbles:

Staff assigned to this position are not allowed to serve more than one week consecutively, and no more than three weeks per year.

Why? The actual description shows no real reason for this, plus finding this many trained people who speak french would be expensive. I know it's the Foundation and we have unlimited resources and stuff but damn that's a lot.

Also, including physical specifications about its height would be a good addition. The first thing I noticed in the picture is that it's freakishly tall. (just visually, if we're assuming the child to be about 1.2 meters, I'd say this thing is about 3.8 meters tall.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I imagined the interviewer tried to get it as "accurate" a transcription as possible, not unlike a court… Stenographer, I believe is the correct term? and I can't honestly say it would sound very much like a six year old girl if I wrote out her enunciating everything she said, yknow?

E: Any suggestions that would appear better and keep narrative flow would be appreciated. It kind of /is/ supposed to tell a story lol, so I want to keep that as intact as possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This.
"Bunch'a", "kinda", "dunno", "horsey". Yeahhh, no.
That part's definitely a flaw, and not a small one, but I still upvoted for the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definite upvote from me. All I will say is that the interview log seems a little overdone, it doesn't seem to be written as a transcript at all, but more as a 'story'. I can't really imagine a Foundation employee writing "'Melia" "shoppin'" "anythin'" especially seeing as it's translated from French.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I has a sad for the extradimensional giant that wants to take care of children and doesn't understand how our universe words.

Take my upvote, good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I…kinda feel sorry for it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting. This does read like it's trying hard, but I think it succeeds. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, I loved that. You have a pretty good sense of building something up, friend. I actually gasped a little when I read the interview.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This, with equal parts creepy and sad, is what I was going for. Glad someone caught it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I think it's time for me to curl up in a corner and cry in fear for a while. Now I get to have nightmares, woo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's trying as hard as it can, but it just can't quite figure us out. Well done.
